findings.impact: Remote attackers can attempt direct privileged logins instead of going through named accounts.
findings.remediation: Set PermitRootLogin no in sshd_config and reload sshd.
findings.title: SSH root login is permitted

findings.impact: Password-based SSH increases exposure to credential stuffing and weak password attacks.
findings.remediation: Prefer key-based SSH and set PasswordAuthentication no after confirming access.
findings.title: SSH password authentication is enabled

findings.impact: Accounts with empty passwords can authenticate over SSH without a credential secret.
findings.remediation: Set PermitEmptyPasswords no in sshd_config and reload sshd.
findings.title: SSH permits empty passwords

findings.impact: Memory corruption exploits become easier when process address layouts are predictable.
findings.remediation: Set kernel.randomize_va_space=2 with sysctl and persist it under /etc/sysctl.d/.
findings.title: Address space layout randomization is disabled

findings.impact: The host can route traffic between interfaces, which broadens blast radius if unintended.
findings.remediation: Set net.ipv4.ip_forward=0 unless this system is intentionally acting as a router.
findings.title: IPv4 forwarding is enabled

findings.impact: Malicious redirects can influence host routing decisions on hostile networks.
findings.remediation: Set net.ipv4.conf.all.accept_redirects=0 unless redirects are explicitly required.
findings.title: IPv4 ICMP redirects are accepted

findings.impact: The host can route IPv6 traffic between interfaces, which may be unintended on non-router systems.
findings.remediation: Set net.ipv6.conf.all.forwarding=0 unless this system is intentionally acting as an IPv6 router.
findings.title: IPv6 forwarding is enabled

findings.impact: Malicious redirects can influence IPv6 routing decisions on hostile networks.
findings.remediation: Set net.ipv6.conf.all.accept_redirects=0 unless redirects are explicitly required.
findings.title: IPv6 ICMP redirects are accepted

findings.impact: Low-address memory mappings can make kernel null-pointer bugs easier to exploit.
findings.remediation: Set vm.mmap_min_addr to at least 65536 unless a legacy workload requires otherwise.
findings.title: Low memory mappings are not protected

findings.impact: Users may be able to create hardlinks to files they do not own, weakening common privilege escalation protections.
findings.remediation: Set fs.protected_hardlinks=1 and persist it under /etc/sysctl.d/.
findings.title: Hardlink protection is disabled

findings.impact: Users may be exposed to symlink race attacks in sticky world-writable directories.
findings.remediation: Set fs.protected_symlinks=1 and persist it under /etc/sysctl.d/.
findings.title: Symlink protection is disabled

findings.impact: Kernel pointer addresses can help attackers bypass kernel exploitation mitigations.
findings.remediation: Set kernel.kptr_restrict=1 or 2 and persist it under /etc/sysctl.d/.
findings.title: Kernel pointer exposure is unrestricted

findings.impact: Unapplied package updates can leave the host exposed to known defects and vulnerabilities.
findings.remediation: Review and apply pending package updates through the system package manager.
findings.title: Package updates are available

findings.impact: Known security fixes have not been applied to this host.
findings.remediation: Prioritize applying pending security updates and restart affected services if required.
findings.title: Security package updates are available

findings.impact: The host may expose services directly without local packet filtering.
findings.remediation: Enable the host firewall or document why perimeter controls are sufficient.
findings.title: Host firewall is disabled
